overview: Duskmourn is a horror-themed set that emphasizes survival mechanics and eerie synergies. The format is medium-speed with strong incentives to be aggressive in certain color pairs while controlling archetypes reward patience. Delirium and room mechanics are central to many strategies, and enchantments matter more than usual due to the Room subtype.

mechanics.name: Rooms
mechanics.description: Room enchantments are double-faced enchantments. You can unlock the second room by paying its cost later. Each side has a different effect.
mechanics.draft_tip: Rooms provide excellent value in longer games. Prioritize rooms that have strong first halves since you may not always unlock the second side. They count as enchantments for synergies.

mechanics.name: Delirium
mechanics.description: Cards with Delirium get a bonus if you have four or more card types in your graveyard.
mechanics.draft_tip: Build your deck with diverse card types to enable Delirium. Artifact creatures, enchantment creatures, and instant/sorcery splits help reach the threshold quickly.

mechanics.name: Survival
mechanics.description: Survival triggers the first time a creature you control attacks while you have less life than your starting total.
mechanics.draft_tip: Survival is naturally enabled in aggressive strategies. Don't be afraid to take some early damage — your survival payoffs will reward it.

mechanics.name: Eerie
mechanics.description: Eerie triggers whenever an enchantment enters the battlefield under your control or you unlock a Room.
mechanics.draft_tip: Eerie rewards enchantment-heavy builds. Rooms trigger Eerie twice — once when cast and again when unlocked. This makes rooms premium in Eerie decks.

mechanics.name: Manifest Dread
mechanics.description: Look at the top two cards of your library, put one face down as a 2/2 creature, and the other into your graveyard.
mechanics.draft_tip: Manifest Dread fuels Delirium by milling cards and creates board presence. The face-down creatures can be turned face up if they're creature cards.

archetypes.name: Eerie Control
archetypes.description: White-Blue focuses on Eerie triggers from enchantments and rooms to generate incremental value and control the board.
archetypes.strategy: Draft rooms and enchantments highly. Use Eerie payoffs to generate card advantage and tempo. Win by outvaluing the opponent with repeated triggers.

archetypes.name: Eerie Tempo
archetypes.description: Blue-Black combines Eerie triggers with tempo plays and removal to dominate the mid-game.
archetypes.strategy: Focus on efficient creatures with Eerie payoffs. Use removal to clear blockers and enchantments to trigger Eerie repeatedly.

archetypes.name: Sacrifice Aggro
archetypes.description: Black-Red uses sacrifice synergies and aggressive creatures to pressure opponents.
archetypes.strategy: Draft cheap aggressive creatures and sacrifice outlets. Use burn spells to clear the way and close out games quickly.

archetypes.name: Delirium Midrange
archetypes.description: Red-Green focuses on reaching Delirium quickly to power up creatures and spells.
archetypes.strategy: Diversify card types to enable Delirium fast. Play efficient midrange threats that become powerful once Delirium is active.

archetypes.name: Survival Aggro
archetypes.description: Green-White uses Survival triggers and go-wide strategies to overwhelm opponents.
archetypes.strategy: Build a curve of efficient creatures. Enable Survival by attacking early and use combat tricks to win races.

archetypes.name: Enchantments Value
archetypes.description: White-Black uses enchantment synergies for removal and value generation.
archetypes.strategy: Prioritize enchantment-based removal. Use enchantment payoffs to generate card advantage while controlling the board.

archetypes.name: Rooms Tempo
archetypes.description: Blue-Red combines rooms with tempo plays and direct damage.
archetypes.strategy: Draft rooms with strong immediate effects. Use burn to clear the path while rooms provide ongoing value.

archetypes.name: Delirium Graveyard
archetypes.description: Black-Green maximizes Delirium with self-mill and graveyard recursion.
archetypes.strategy: Self-mill aggressively to fuel Delirium. Use recursion to grind out opponents and powerful Delirium payoffs as finishers.

archetypes.name: Survival Aggro
archetypes.description: Red-White goes aggressive with Survival triggers and efficient beaters.
archetypes.strategy: Curve out aggressively. Use combat tricks and burn to push through damage and trigger Survival.

archetypes.name: Manifest Value
archetypes.description: Green-Blue uses Manifest Dread to generate card advantage and board presence.
archetypes.strategy: Use Manifest Dread to fill the board while milling for value. Turn creatures face up for tempo and use green's size advantage.

general_tips: Rooms are premium picks — they provide two-for-one value and trigger Eerie twice.
Delirium is easier to enable than it looks. Prioritize artifact creatures and enchantment creatures for type diversity.
The format rewards proactive strategies. Pure control without a win condition struggles.
Enchantment-based removal (like Sheltered by Ghosts) is premium because it enables Eerie.
Survival decks want to trade life for tempo early — don't be afraid to take damage.
Manifest Dread is excellent because it develops your board while fueling your graveyard.
